title: astragalus lentiginosus var. micans
intro: Astragalus lentiginosus var. micans, also known as spotted milkvetch, is a plant species within the legume family, Fabaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries. The medicinal properties of Astragalus lentiginosus var. micans include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to prepare herbal remedies, such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, to support immune function, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma.
